2604.07100.title: STRIDE-ED: A Strategy-Grounded Stepwise Reasoning Framework for Empathetic Dialogue Systems
2604.07100.paper_summary: Empathetic dialogue requires not only recognizing a user's emotional state but also making strategy-aware, context-sensitive decisions throughout response generation. However, the lack of a comprehensive empathy strategy framework, explicit task-aligned multi-stage reasoning, and high-quality strategy-aware data fundamentally limits existing approaches, preventing them from effectively modeling empathetic dialogue as a complex, multi-stage cognitive and decision-making process. To address these challenges, we propose STRIDE-ED, a STRategy-grounded, Interpretable, and DEep reasoning framework that models Empathetic Dialogue through structured, strategy-conditioned reasoning. To support effective learning, we develop a strategy-aware data refinement pipeline integrating LLM-based annotation, multi-model consistency-weighted evaluation, and dynamic sampling to construct high-quality training data aligned with empathetic strategies. Furthermore, we adopt a two-stage training paradigm that combines supervised fine-tuning with multi-objective reinforcement learning to better align model behaviors with target emotions, empathetic strategies, and response formats. Extensive experiments demonstrate that STRIDE-ED generalizes across diverse open-source LLMs and consistently outperforms existing methods on both automatic metrics and human evaluations.
2604.07100.paper_summary_zh: 同理心對話不僅需要識別使用者的情感狀態，還需要在回應生成過程中做出策略意識的、情境敏感的決策。<lb/>然而，缺乏全面的同理心策略框架、明確的任務對齊多階段推理，以及高品質的策略意識數據，根本上限制了現有的方法，使其無法有效地將同理心對話建模為一個複雜的、多階段的認知和決策過程。<lb/>為了解決這些挑戰，我們提出了STRIDE-ED，一個基於策略的、可解釋的、深度推理框架，通過結構化的、策略條件的推理來建模同理心對話。<lb/>為了支持有效的學習，我們開發了一個策略意識數據精煉管道，整合了基於LLM的標註、多模型一致性加權評估和動態抽樣，以構建與同理心策略對齊的高品質訓練數據。<lb/>此外，我們採用了兩階段的訓練範式，結合了監督微調和多目標強化學習，以更好地使模型行為與目標情感、同理心策略和回應格式對齊。<lb/>廣泛的實驗表明，STRIDE-ED在各種開源LLM上具有良好的泛化能力，並在自動指標和人類評估中始終超越現有方法。
2604.07100.author: Hongru Ji et.al.
2604.07100.authors: Hongru Ji, Yuyin Fan, Meng Zhao, Xianghua Li, Lianwei Wu, Chao Gao
